{"id":"project-LICHEN-zrh2","title":"PHY: Adaptive spreading factor","description":"## Problem

LICHEN uses SF10 for all traffic. But nearby nodes could use SF7 (10x faster airtime), freeing channel capacity.

SF10 60-byte packet: ~500ms airtime
SF7 60-byte packet: ~50ms airtime

## Hardware requirement

**Works on ALL hardware.** Any LoRa chip supports SF7-SF12.

## Solution

Adapt SF based on link quality:
1. Start at SF10 (safe default)
2. If SNR is high (link is strong), try lower SF
3. If packets fail, increase SF
4. Per-neighbor SF tracking

## Algorithm

on_rx(packet, snr):
  if snr > SNR_THRESHOLD_HIGH:
    neighbor_sf[src] = max(SF7, neighbor_sf[src] - 1)
  elif snr < SNR_THRESHOLD_LOW:
    neighbor_sf[src] = min(SF12, neighbor_sf[src] + 1)

on_tx(packet, dst):
  sf = neighbor_sf.get(dst, SF10)  # default SF10
  transmit(packet, sf)

## Tradeoffs

- Receiver must know which SF to listen on
- Solution: RX window scans all SFs, or announce includes TX SF
- More complexity but potentially 10x capacity for nearby nodes

## Acceptance criteria

- Spec defines adaptive SF algorithm and signaling
- Implementations track per-neighbor SF
- Dense deployment shows reduced airtime","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","owner":"me@mark.atwood.name","created_at":"2026-07-16T18:21:42.793624Z","created_by":"Mark Atwood","updated_at":"2026-07-16T18:21:42.793624Z","labels":["phy","scalability"]}
